items.title: A look at every player who had a positive EPM in the 2026 NBA playoffs categorized by position.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
A look at every player who had a positive EPM in the 2026 NBA playoffs categorized by position. 

Data: Dunksandthrees.com 

Glossary; Estimated Plus-Minus (EPM) is an all-in-one player metric that predicts a player's contribution to the team in points per 100 possessions.

images.alt:
Alt text:

"Bar chart titled 'Estimated Plus-Minus by Position,' subtitled '2026 NBA Playoffs, players with a positive playoff EPM, darker equals higher EPM.' Five columns for PG, SG, SF, PF, and C list players ranked highest to lowest EPM, with bar shading darkening as values rise.

PG: Jalen Brunson +3.2, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander +3.1, Dylan Harper +2.9, Donte DiVincenzo +2.4, Cade Cunningham +1.7, Jose Alvarado +1.7, Mike Conley +1.2, James Harden +1.1, Marcus Smart +0.8, Payton Pritchard +0.6, De'Aaron Fox +0.4, Tyrese Maxey +0.0.

SG: Ajay Mitchell +4.8, Cason Wallace +3.1, Stephon Castle +1.1, RJ Barrett +0.9, VJ Edgecombe +0.5, Donovan Mitchell +0.2.

SF: Alex Caruso +3.8, Mikal Bridges +2.6, Ausar Thompson +2.5, Devin Vassell +2.3, Josh Hart +1.9, Tari Eason +1.1, Franz Wagner +1.0, Paul George +0.5, LeBron James +0.5.

PF: OG Anunoby +3.7, Scottie Barnes +3.2, Jayson Tatum +3.1, Julian Champagnie +1.2, Paolo Banchero +1.1, Dillon Brooks +0.9, Jalen Williams +0.3, Rui Hachimura +0.3, Evan Mobley +0.2, Dean Wade +0.1.

C: Victor Wembanyama +9.4, Karl-Anthony Towns +8.0, Paul Reed +3.2, Isaiah Hartenstein +2.4, Jarrett Allen +2.3, Chet Holmgren +2.0, Robert Williams III +1.7, Nikola Jokic +1.2, Wendell Carter Jr. +0.9, Luka Garza +0.3.

Footer: Data from DunksAndThrees.com, visualization by HoopInformatics.bsky.social."

items.title: LeBron should stay in the league until he’s washed and then fulfill a Haslem role for the Cavs. Because realistically LeBron could play for 6 more years before he’s completely washed and unplayable.
items.author: PooPoox
items.body_excerpt: LeBron will retire before he’s completely washed but if he played until he couldn’t anymore he could 1. Make his all time stats even more untouchable 2. Compete on the court and help guide the Cavs future

items.title: Hollinger POTD Michael Jordan. Bio from MJ's comeback for the WAS in at age 38. He still could create mid-rangers but wore down under heavy minutes. His usage at 36% remains even higher than LBJ at the same age but knee issues knocked him out by March.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollinger POTD Michael Jordan. Bio from MJ's comeback for the WAS in at age 38. He still could create mid-rangers but wore down under heavy minutes. His usage at 36% remains even higher than LBJ at the same age but knee issues knocked him out by March.
